what_it_does: Defines ConceptV CSS design tokens, typography presets, scoped defaults, surface-zone color roles, palette variants, and helper classes for applying zone backgrounds and border colors.
observations: Imports Sora, DM Sans, and JetBrains Mono from Google Fonts.
Declares root-level tokens for paper surfaces, ink colors, foreground aliases, brand accents, semantic statuses, borders, shadows, radii, spacing, type, motion, and layout primitives.
Keeps legacy background and foreground aliases with comments warning not to remove them.
Provides semantic type classes for display, headings, body, captions, eyebrow text, mono text, screen titles, and inline accents.
Adds .cv-scope defaults for typography, background, heading styles, paragraph handling, and code/pre fonts.
Defines a surface-zone registry using pigment variables, intensity controls, and color-mix(in oklch) to derive surface, strong, edge, and ink roles.
Includes cooler and warmer palette variants by overriding pigment tokens.
Provides helper classes that apply zone surfaces and border colors.
standouts: The zone system derives multiple semantic roles from pigment variables and global intensity controls.
Gold is explicitly scoped to Vi, active decisions, selected states, emphasis, and actions.
The file documents intended visual behavior directly beside the tokens.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties as the token interface.
Organizes sections with large comment banners and explanatory inline comments.
Uses warm off-white surfaces and warm ink instead of pure black for the core palette.
Uses prefix families such as --paper, --ink, --fg, --accent, --status, --border, --shadow, --r, --s, --font, --tracking, --layout, --pig, and --zone.
Spacing tokens are documented as an 8px rhythm while also including 4px, 12px, and 20px intermediate values.
concerns: Several spacing aliases share identical values, such as --s-7 and --s-8 both being 32px, and --s-9 and --s-10 both being 40px.
The comment says spacing is a strict 8px rhythm, but the scale includes 4px, 12px, and 20px values.
The surface-zone helper comment mentions .zone-ink children, but no .zone-ink selector is defined in this file.
Modern color-mix(in oklch) usage may require target environments with support for that CSS color syntax.
notes: This is a design-system token and utility stylesheet centered on warm paper surfaces, typography presets, and semantic tonal zones.
questions: Should duplicate spacing values remain as named aliases for semantic use?
Is a .zone-ink helper expected to be defined in this file or elsewhere?
Are the Google Fonts imports acceptable for every surface that imports this token file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it acts as a shared colors-and-type token layer with scoped typography defaults and surface-zone utilities.
apparent_themes: Gold as decision and action accent
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to keep multiple ConceptV surfaces visually synchronized through shared tokens while making semantic zones adjustable through a small set of pigment and intensity controls.
novelty: The surface-zone registry is distinct in deriving zone roles from named pigments and global intensity knobs rather than hardcoding every zone color independently.
contribution: Contributes a warm paper-based token set, typography presets, legacy aliases, semantic zone pigments, adjustable derived zone roles, and utility classes that a fused system would keep as a reusable visual substrate.
summary_for_embedding: CSS design-system file defining ConceptV color tokens, typography presets, scoped defaults, spacing, shadows, layout primitives, semantic surface-zone roles, palette variants, and zone helper classes.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for synchronizing ConceptV deck, UI kit, and mock surfaces around a shared warm paper visual language with adjustable semantic zoning.
